author: Guruguhan Iyer
passage: "Shunya Vedana … Shunya Vedana … Shunya Vedana …". The chanting never stops at 7, Srujan Nivas. It looks like an ordinary flat, but is it? Ramana and Meenakshi move into their new home right after their wedding. Three days later, his parents visit them, drift off under the ceiling fan, and die in their sleep. Two decades later, Meenakshi meets the same fate. Their son Venkat realises his family's living the last phase of their lives over and over. Shunya and Vedana, the self-proclaimed Gods, promise liberation in exchange for Venkat's complete surrender. Will he yield or is there something far bigger at play? The story is set in Matunga, Mumbai. The restaurants, shops and temples mentioned are real.
